title: Zoto Eval Non-Skill Case (declarative | runner)
description: Canonical case shape for every non-skill primitive eval (commands / agents / hooks / central JSON files). The discriminator is the `runner` field: when present, the case is a *runner case* that delegates execution to a typed `.test.ts` file and skips declarative grading. When absent, the case is a *declarative case* that the harness grades through assertions + graders. Skill `evals/evals.json` files keep their Cursor-spec shape (`skill_name` + `evals[]`) and are out of scope for this schema. Consumers MUST register `case-meta.schema.json` with their Ajv instance before compiling this schema so the `_meta` `$ref` resolves.

DeclarativeCase.description: Declarative case — the harness sends `prompt` to the agent under test, then evaluates the response against `assertions[]` and the optional `graders[]` list. MUST NOT carry `runner` or `parameters` (those promote the case to the RunnerCase branch).

id.description: Stable case identifier within the file. Strings are recommended; integers are tolerated for legacy compatibility.

prompt.description: Realistic user / agent turn sent to the model. Placeholder tokens (TODO, TBD, FIXME, <placeholder>, ...) are rejected by the runtime `validateEnriched` guard.

assertions.description: Natural-language requirements the response must satisfy. The implicit assertion-derived rubric is judged by the configured `judgeModel`.

assertion_patterns.description: Optional cheap regex patterns evaluated before the LLM judge — short-circuit the expensive rubric when a structural assertion fails.

oneOf.description: Legacy bare-string tag — no-op at runtime, kept for backwards compatibility.
Object grader dispatched by `type`. See `engine/case.ts#DeclarativeGraderConfig` for the typed shape per kind.

follow_ups.description: Legacy synthetic follow-up turns (`agent.send` per entry). `interactions.answers` takes precedence when both are present.

interactions.description: Scripted AskQuestion turns for interactive cases. The harness routes these through the AskQuestion bridge.

RunnerCase.description: Runner case — execution is delegated to a typed `.test.ts` file whose default export implements `RunnerFn = (params: RunnerParams) => Promise<RunnerResult>`. The harness skips declarative grading entirely and records the runner's `RunnerResult` via the existing `reportCase` API. The `runner` string is resolved relative to the JSON file's directory (`new URL(runner, jsonFileUrl)`). Declarative fields (`prompt`, `assertions`, `graders`, `fixtures`, `expected_filesystem`, `expected_output`) are forbidden — those belong to the DeclarativeCase branch.

runner.description: Relative path to the `.test.ts` runner from the JSON file's directory. MUST end in `.test.ts` so the loader can distinguish runner targets from arbitrary modules. Subtask 03 imports it via `await import(new URL(runner, jsonFileUrl))`.

parameters.description: Free-form typed payload passed to the runner as `RunnerParams.parameters`. The runner module is responsible for validating its own shape — this schema only requires that `parameters` is an object.
